AQA.name: Paper 1
AQA.title: The Study of Religions: Beliefs, Teachings and Practices

assessmentObjectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of religion and belief
Analyse and evaluate aspects of religion and belief, including their significance, influence and impact

topics.name: The Nature of God
topics.description: God as omnipotent, loving and just; the problem of evil and suffering; the oneness of God and the Trinity

topics.name: Creation
topics.description: Different Christian beliefs about creation including Genesis 1:1-3 and John 1:1-3; the role of Word and Spirit

topics.name: The Incarnation
topics.description: Beliefs about the incarnation and Jesus as the Son of God; fully human and fully divine

topics.name: The Crucifixion and Resurrection
topics.description: Beliefs about the crucifixion, resurrection and ascension of Jesus and their significance

topics.name: Sin and Salvation
topics.description: Original sin; the means of salvation including law, grace and Spirit; atonement

topics.name: The Afterlife
topics.description: Different Christian beliefs about the afterlife: resurrection, judgement, heaven and hell

topics.name: Forms of Worship
topics.description: Liturgical, non-liturgical and informal worship; private worship; the role of the Bible

topics.name: Prayer
topics.description: The significance of prayer; the Lord's Prayer; set prayers and informal prayer

topics.name: Sacraments: Baptism and Eucharist
topics.description: The meaning of sacrament; infant and believers' baptism; Holy Communion and its significance

topics.name: Pilgrimage
topics.description: The role and importance of pilgrimage; contrasting examples of Lourdes and Iona

topics.name: Christmas and Easter
topics.description: The celebrations of Christmas and Easter and their importance for Christians in Britain today

topics.name: The Role of the Church
topics.description: The Church in the local community; food banks and street pastors; mission and evangelism

topics.name: The Worldwide Church
topics.description: Working for reconciliation; responding to persecution; the work of CAFOD, Christian Aid or Tearfund

topics.name: The Six Articles and Five Roots
topics.description: The six articles of faith in Sunni Islam; the five roots of Usul ad-Din in Shi'a Islam; key similarities and differences

topics.name: Tawhid: The Oneness of God
topics.description: The nature of God: omnipotence, beneficence, mercy, fairness and justice; immanence and transcendence

topics.name: Angels
topics.description: The nature and role of angels including Jibril and Mika'il

topics.name: Predestination and Free Will
topics.description: Predestination and human freedom and its relationship to the Day of Judgement

topics.name: Akhirah: Life After Death
topics.description: Human responsibility and accountability; resurrection; heaven and hell

topics.name: Risalah: Prophethood
topics.description: The role and importance of Adam, Ibrahim and Muhammad as prophets; the holy books

topics.name: The Imamate
topics.description: The role and significance of the imamate in Shi'a Islam

topics.name: The Five Pillars
topics.description: Shahadah: declaration of faith and its place in Muslim practice

topics.name: Salah: Prayer
topics.description: How and why Muslims pray; times, directions, wudu, rak'ahs; Jummah; Sunni and Shi'a differences

topics.name: Sawm: Fasting
topics.description: The role and significance of fasting during Ramadan; origins, duties, exceptions, Night of Power

topics.name: Zakah and Khums
topics.description: The role and significance of giving alms; how and why it is given; Khums in Shi'a Islam

topics.name: Hajj: Pilgrimage
topics.description: The role and significance of pilgrimage to Makkah; Ka'aba, Mina, Arafat, Muzdalifah

topics.name: Jihad
topics.description: Greater and lesser jihad; origins, influence and conditions for the declaration of lesser jihad

topics.name: Islamic Festivals
topics.description: Id-ul-Adha, Id-ul-Fitr and Ashura; their origins, meanings and importance in Britain today

topics.name: Human Sexuality and Relationships
topics.description: Heterosexual and same-sex relationships; sexual relationships before and outside of marriage

topics.name: Marriage and Divorce
topics.description: The nature and purpose of marriage; same-sex marriage and cohabitation; divorce and remarrying

topics.name: Contraception and Family Planning
topics.description: Religious teachings on contraception; contrasting Christian and Muslim perspectives

topics.name: The Nature and Purpose of Families
topics.description: The role of parents and children; extended and nuclear families; procreation, stability, faith education

topics.name: Gender Equality and Prejudice
topics.description: The roles of men and women; gender equality; gender prejudice and discrimination including examples

topics.name: The Origins of the Universe
topics.description: Religious teachings about origins; the Big Bang theory; the relationship between scientific and religious views

topics.name: Stewardship and the Environment
topics.description: The duty to protect the world; stewardship, dominion, responsibility, awe and wonder; use and abuse of the environment

topics.name: The Origins of Human Life
topics.description: Religious teachings about the origins of human life; evolution and religious views

topics.name: Abortion
topics.description: Sanctity of life and quality of life; situations when the mother's life is at risk; contrasting perspectives

topics.name: Euthanasia and the Afterlife
topics.description: Beliefs about euthanasia; beliefs about death and an afterlife; their impact on the value of human life

topics.name: Animal Rights and Experimentation
topics.description: The use and abuse of animals; animal experimentation; use of animals for food; contrasting perspectives

strands.name: Theme D: Religion, Peace and Conflict

topics.name: Peace, Justice and Reconciliation
topics.description: The meaning and significance of peace, justice, forgiveness and reconciliation

topics.name: Violence, Terrorism and War
topics.description: Violent protest; terrorism; reasons for war including greed, self-defence and retaliation

topics.name: Just War and Holy War
topics.description: The just war theory and its criteria; holy war; contrasting Christian and Muslim perspectives

topics.name: Nuclear Weapons and Pacifism
topics.description: Nuclear weapons and deterrence; weapons of mass destruction; pacifism; religious peace-making

strands.name: Theme E: Religion, Crime and Punishment

topics.name: Good, Evil and the Causes of Crime
topics.description: Good and evil intentions and actions; reasons for crime: poverty, mental health, greed, opposition to unjust law

topics.name: Types of Crime
topics.description: Hate crimes, theft and murder; views about people who break the law for different reasons

topics.name: Aims of Punishment
topics.description: Retribution, deterrence and reformation; religious views on the purpose of punishment

topics.name: The Treatment of Criminals
topics.description: Prison, corporal punishment and community service; religious perspectives on treatment of offenders

topics.name: Forgiveness and the Death Penalty
topics.description: Religious teachings on forgiveness; the death penalty; ethical arguments based on utility and sanctity of life
